title: Garland appoints special counsel to investigate Biden docs
NSA director pushes Congress to renew surveillance powers
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top U.S. intelligence official on Thursday urged Congress to renew sweeping powers granted to American spy agencies to surveil and examine communications, saying they were critical to stopping terrorism, cyberattacks and other threats.

title: DeSantis tested on immigration as he weighs 2024 candidacy
shortDescription: TAVERNIER, Fla. (AP) — Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis sent dozens of immigrants from Texas to an island off the Massachusetts coast last year in a high-profile effort to highlight illegal immigration on the eve of the midterm elections.

title: Trump to hold 1st public campaign event in South Carolina
shortDescription: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Former President Donald Trump is planning to hold the first public campaign event of his 2024 White House bid in the early-voting state of South Carolina.

title: Prosecutor: Proud Boys attacked ‘heart’ of democracy on 1/6
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio and four lieutenants led a coordinated attack on “the heart of our democracy” in a desperate attempt to keep Donald Trump in the White House, a federal prosecutor said Thursday at the start of their seditious conspiracy trial.

title: House votes to block China from buying oil from US reserves
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Republican-controlled House on Thursday voted to block oil from the country's emergency stockpile from going to China.

title: Turkey summons Swedish envoy over ‘heinous’ protest action
shortDescription: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey has summoned the Swedish ambassador to convey Ankara’s displeasure after a mannequin depicting Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan was hung outside the city hall in Stockholm, state broadcaster TRT reported.

title: Late Pentagon chief Ash Carter honored as ‘force of nature’
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden honored the late Ash Carter, the former defense secretary who opened the way for women to fight in combat and for transgender personnel to serve, as a “force of nature” at a memorial service on Thursday at Washington National Cathedral.

title: Nebraska governor names predecessor to US Senate seat
shortDescription: LINCOLN, Neb. (AP) — In one of his first acts as Nebraska’s governor, Republican Jim Pillen named his predecessor to the state’s vacant U.S. Senate seat on Thursday.Pillen surprised no one in naming fellow Republican Pete Ricketts to the seat vacated Sunday by Ben Sasse.

title: Dem lawmakers urge Biden to void visa of Brazil’s Bolsonaro
shortDescription: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — A group of 46 Democratic lawmakers sent a letter to President Joe Biden on Thursday demanding former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro's diplomatic visa be canceled in the wake of the rampage in Brazil's capital by his supporters.

title: Activist who ran for Belarusian presidency in 2020 detained
shortDescription: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — An opposition politician who ran against authoritarian Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko in the controversial 2020 presidential election has been arrested in Minsk, a human rights group said Thursday.

title: New York Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand launches reelection bid
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand of New York is kicking off her reelection campaign.The 56-year-old sent out an email to supporters on Thursday announcing her 2024 bid. “I wanted you to hear it first,” the email said.

title: Czech ex-PM Babis favorite in presidential vote’s 1st round
shortDescription: PRAGUE (AP) — Populist billionaire Andrej Babis leads a field of eight candidates hoping to succeed Milos Zeman in the largely ceremonial but prestigious post of the Czech president.Babis, a former prime minister, was recently acquitted in a fraud trial, boosting his chances of winning in the first round of the presidential election in voting on Friday and Saturday.

title: Ally of ex-Pakistan PM wins confidence vote in blow to gov’t
shortDescription: LAHORE, Pakistan (AP) — A political ally of Pakistan's former prime minister won a vote of confidence early Thursday in the Punjab Assembly, a major blow to the federal government, officials said.Pervez Elahi secured 186 votes in the 371-seat Punjab Assembly to remain the chief minister in Punjab, weeks after the provincial governor, Balighur Rehman, sacked him.

title: Who is Robert Hur? Special counsel tapped to probe Biden documents
shortDescription: Robert Hur is a former U.S. attorney in Maryland who served as a principal associate deputy attorney general during the Trump administration.

title: Former GOP Nebraska Gov. Pete Ricketts to fill seat vacated by Ben Sasse
shortDescription: Ricketts, who will be sworn in later this month, cited government spending and national defense as the top issues he would pursue in Congress.

title: Robert Hur named as special counsel to investigate Biden classified docs
shortDescription: Attorney General Merrick Garland appointed Robert Hur as a special counsel to review the handling of classified documents related to President Biden on Jan. 12, 2023.

title: More classified documents found at Biden's home
shortDescription: Attorney General Merrick Garland says he is appointing a special counsel to investigate Biden's classified documents.

title: Second set of Biden docs found in president's Delaware garage
shortDescription: The records were recovered after Biden's lawyers conducted searches of the president's two Delaware residences.

title: Questions remain about Biden classified docs as second batch is found
shortDescription: The review move towards an inflection point with many questions unanswered. President Joe Biden has maintained the matter was handled appropriately.

title: First Lady Jill Biden has two cancerous lesions removed: White House
shortDescription: First Lady Jill Biden had two cancerous lesions removed, the White House said. One was over her right eye and the other on the left side of her chest.

title: White House won't say when Biden learned about classified documents
shortDescription: The White House pointed to an ongoing Justice Department review, declining to answer when President Joe Biden knew about the classified documents.

title: Second batch of Biden documents located, reports say
shortDescription: The disclosure comes a day after Biden told reporters he was "surprised" by the discovery of the first batch of documents.

title: Katie Porter is running for senate. Get to know the oversite maven
shortDescription: Congresswoman Katie Porter has announced her run for U.S. Senate. Here's what you need to know about the California representative.

title: Republicans call for on George Santos to resign
shortDescription: New York Republicans are demanding Rep. George Santos resign after he admitted to lying about his education, work experience and being Jewish.

title: Lawmakers vow probe of FAA outage; GOP blames Biden administration
shortDescription: Congress plans to investigate the FAA"s computer outage that cancelled more than 7,300 flights and sparked outrage from Republican lawmakers.

title: NY Republicans urge Santos to resign, McCarthy says it's up to the voters
shortDescription: Speaker Kevin McCarthy and Majority Leader Steve Scalise told reporters they had not seen the call from New York Republicans for Santos to step down.

title: Biden orders FAA outage investigation
shortDescription: President Joe Biden ordered an investigation into the cause of a Federal Aviation Administration outage that caused thousands of flight delays.

title: Supreme Court keeps New York's expansive handgun law in place for now
shortDescription: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul signed the law after a Supreme Court ruling in June made it far easier for many Americans to carry handguns in public.

title: Biden says he was surprised to learn there were classified documents in his office
shortDescription: Biden addressed the classified documents found in an old office while in Mexico City, where he met with the leaders of Canada and Mexico.

title: McCarthy's 'secret deal,' Santos, classified docs: new Congress takeaways
shortDescription: Democrats and Republicans in Congress are beginning the new session in turmoil over a "secret deal," committee positions, Trump, Biden and more.

title: Shipping container barrier to cost AZ taxpayers $200M
shortDescription: Arizona taxpayers will foot the bill to put up and take down former Gov. Doug Ducey's shipping container border barrier.

title: Mexican president thanks Biden for not adding to border wall
shortDescription: Mexican President Andrés Manuel López Obrador thanked President Joe Biden for not adding to the wall along the U.S.-Mexican border.

title: Biden comments on classified documents found
shortDescription: President Joe Biden said he's cooperating with the National Archives after classified documents were found in an old office in Washington, D.C.

title: Investigating the investigators: New GOP House panels to probe DOJ, China
shortDescription: Republicans are keeping their campaign promises after winning the House majority, launching investigations into Biden and his administration.

title: OnPolitics: Biden's documents vs. Trump's
shortDescription: The White House contrasted his returning documents voluntarily with Trump holding his records.

title: Ex-Trump Organization CFO Allen Weisselberg sentenced to 5 months
shortDescription: After helping prosecutors win criminal tax fraud convictions of two Trump companies, the ex-Trump Organization CFO gets lenient sentence under deal.

title: What we know about President Joe Biden's classified documents
shortDescription: Classified records found in an office once used by Joe Biden echoes the discovery at Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago. What we know about the two cases.

title: GOP leaders seeking Biden investigation
shortDescription: Democrats and Republicans have two different opinions on how to proceed after classified records were found in an office once used by Joe Biden.

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: House Passes Bill That Could Subject Some Abortion Doctors to Prosecution
shortDescription: As part of an anti-abortion rights effort, Republicans pushed through a bill laying out criminal penalties for doctors who fail to resuscitate babies born after an attempted abortion.By Annie Karni

title: Here Are All the Ways Republicans Plan to Investigate Biden
shortDescription: House Republicans are preparing a cascade of investigations, some overlapping, into the Biden administration and its policies. Right-wing lawmakers have said the ultimate goal is to impeach the president.By Luke Broadwater

title: The Special Counsel Investigating Trump Faces Headwinds Few Others Have
shortDescription: Jack Smith’s challenges include a newly empowered Republican House majority and the recent disclosures of President Biden’s retention of classified documents.By Katie Benner, Adam Goldman, Glenn Thrush and Maggie Haberman

title: Hunter Biden’s Tangled Tale Comes Front and Center
shortDescription: Federal prosecutors could decide soon whether to indict the president’s son on tax and gun charges, and he faces a fresh round of hostile congressional hearings. But a close look at his story shows that it differs in important ways from the narrative promoted by Republicans.By Adam Entous, Michael S. Schmidt and Katie Benner
